title: Light Boil Pork Stir-fry
description: A quick-fix Chinese dish featuring ground pork and a light broth for vegetarians or those following a pescatarian diet.

ingredients.name: Vegetable Broth Base (optional)
ingredients.notes: For vegetarians or vegans, a vegetable broth can be used instead of a pork broth.

ingredients.name: Oyster Sauce
ingredients.notes: Or Hoisin sauce for a sweeter flavor, optional

ingredients.name: Soy Sauce
ingredients.notes: For added umami and protein boost

ingredients.name: Cornstarch Slurry
ingredients.amount: 1 tablespoon cornstarch mixed with 1.5 tablespoons water

instructions: Heat a pan over medium heat, add vegetable broth base and ground pork, cook until pork is lightly browned.
Add minced garlic and stir for about 30 seconds, then mix in oyster sauce or hoisin sauce (if used), soy sauce, and cornstarch slurry.
Stir continuously to prevent clumping of ingredients. Bring to a simmer and let the mixture thicken slightly.
Remove from heat once the broth has thickened as desired, stir in finely chopped green onions before serving.
